name: Chili Elizabeth Taylor
recipeIngredient: 1 cup canned tomatoes, drained and seeded
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large skillet, sauté onion and garlic in oil until soft and translucent, not browned.
2. Add ground beef and cook until the meat is no longer red.
3. Stir in tomatoes, tomato puree, chili powder, Worcestershire sauce, red wine vinegar, hot pepper sauce, bay leaves, salt, and chili flakes.
4. Let simmer, stirring occasionally, for 1 hour. Adjust seasonings to taste (the chili can be refrigerated until ready to use).
5. Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C) and transfer the chili to a two-quart baking pan or ovenproof casserole.
6. Pour or spoon cornbread batter over the top of the chili.
7. Bake at 375°F (190°C) for 20-30 minutes, until the cornbread is baked through. The baking time will depend on the depth of the batter.
8. Serve chili with a side dish of black beans, if desired. Makes four servings.
